title: Option gallery decision brief
summary: **Compare-first conclusion:** use the generated report path unless the choice needs a custom disposable editor.

intent.primaryQuestion: Which HTML artifact shape should the next agent choose?
intent.decision: Start with an option gallery when the user is choosing between alternatives.
successCriteria: The chosen path can be copied as a next action.

claims.statement: Option gallery reports lower decision cost when the unresolved work is choosing between alternatives.
knownLimits: Use a concise Markdown answer instead when there is only one obvious path.

sections.title: Option gallery
sections.summary: Compare alternatives in one reading plane before implementation starts.

options.name: Generated decision matrix
points: Best for implementation approaches, rollout options, and planning choices.
Keeps recommendation, tradeoffs, assumptions, and next action in one file.
Uses existing generator and validator without custom HTML.

options.name: Research explainer
points: Best when the reader first needs background, citations, or a module map.
Add tabs, glossary, and diagrams only when they shorten understanding.
Avoid it when the decision is already clear.

options.name: Disposable export editor
points: Best when the user needs to sort, tune, or toggle a small decision surface.
Must export Markdown, JSON, or diff as visible text.
Must not write files, call network APIs, or persist secrets.

sections.title: Selection rules
sections.summary: Use the smallest artifact that performs the needed HTML-only job.

sections.title: Next action
items: Choose `option-gallery` when unresolved work is comparing alternatives.
Choose `module-map` or `flow-drilldown` when structure is the hard part.
Choose `disposable-export-editor` only when a local export tightens the user loop.

evidence.label: HTML effectiveness gallery
evidence.value: 20 self-contained HTML examples grouped by work type

verification.label: Fixture scope
verification.detail: Exercises existing decision-matrix, data-table, actions, claims, and source evidence paths.

nextActions: Validate the generated HTML with validate-interaction.mjs.
